qa_pairs.question: What happens if my CCV on file is older than 90 days?
qa_pairs.answer: If your CCV on file is older than 90 days, you will be required to submit a new CCV with your Resource Allocation Competitions (RAC) application.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my CCV on file is older than 3 months and I need to submit a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: You should go to the CCV website, make changes as needed to the Alliance CCV template, submit it when ready, then enter the new CCV confirmation code on CCDB and click 'Update CCV'.

qa_pairs.question: If my CCV was uploaded within the last three months, what information will CCDB show?
qa_pairs.answer: If your CCV was uploaded within the last three months, CCDB will show your latest CCV available on file, the date when it was last submitted, and your selection of publications enabled by Alliance resources.

qa_pairs.question: Is submitting a new CCV optional for a RAC application if my current CCV on file was updated within the last 90 days?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if the version of your CCV on file was updated within the previous 90 days, submitting a new one (e.g., because new information was added to the CCV since the spring) with your RAC application is optional.
